title: UK Structured Products Risk Assessment
content:
This risk assessment framework governs the recommendation and distribution of structured products to Meridian Wealth Partners clients, ensuring full compliance with FCA COBS 9.2 suitability requirements and PRIIPs Regulation (UK) disclosure obligations. All advisor-level staff (Tier 2 and above) must adhere to these protocols when considering structured product allocations within client portfolios.

Structured products present complex risk-return profiles that require sophisticated client assessment. These instruments typically combine fixed-income securities with embedded derivatives, creating exposure to underlying assets such as equity indices, commodities, or currency baskets. Capital protection levels vary significantly, ranging from 0% to 90% of initial investment, with many products offering no principal protection whatsoever.

Client suitability assessment must encompass multiple dimensions. Minimum liquid net worth requirements stand at £250,000, excluding primary residence, with structured product allocations capped at 15% of total investible assets for most clients. Exception protocols permit up to 25% allocation for ultra-high-net-worth individuals (£2 million+ liquid assets) demonstrating sophisticated investment experience. All clients must acknowledge understanding that structured products may result in total capital loss, particularly those linked to volatile underlying assets.

Risk categorisation follows our five-tier framework. Level 1 products offer full capital protection with modest upside participation (typically 60-80% of underlying performance). Level 5 products, classified as aggressive risk, provide leveraged exposure with potential returns exceeding 200% but carry substantial downside risk. Reverse convertible notes and barrier options frequently fall within this category, requiring enhanced due diligence and documentation.

Regulatory compliance mandates provision of PRIIPs Key Information Documents (KIDs) prior to client recommendation. These documents must clearly articulate performance scenarios, costs, and risk indicators on the standardised 1-7 scale. Our internal risk assessment often diverges from PRIIPs classifications, requiring additional client disclosure when our assessment indicates higher risk levels than regulatory documentation suggests.

Counterparty risk assessment remains paramount, given structured products' unsecured nature. We maintain approved issuer lists encompassing major investment banks with minimum A- credit ratings from two recognised agencies. Concentration limits restrict single-issuer exposure to 40% of client's structured product allocation, reducing bankruptcy risk. Recent market volatility has prompted enhanced monitoring of issuer credit default swap spreads, with positions reviewed monthly rather than quarterly.

Liquidity considerations demand careful evaluation, as secondary markets for structured products remain limited. Typical bid-offer spreads range from 3-8% of notional value, with wider spreads during market stress periods. Clients must demonstrate ability to hold positions to maturity, typically 3-6 years, without requiring premature liquidation. Emergency liquidity needs should be met through alternative portfolio components.

Documentation requirements include completed structured product questionnaire, signed risk acknowledgement form, and detailed suitability report justifying the recommendation. All communications must emphasise that past performance indicators do not guarantee future results and that structured products may underperform traditional investments during certain market conditions. Quarterly portfolio reviews must specifically address structured product performance and continued suitability within evolving client circumstances.
